metadata.description: 类高级功能: English translation: - `classmethod`: A class method receives the class as implicit first argument, just like an instance method receives the instance. To declare a class method, use this idiom: ```python from functools import wraps def classmethod(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(cls, *args, **kwargs): return func(cls, *args, **kwargs) return wrapper # Example usage: class MyClass: @classmethod def my_class_method(cls): print(f"Called my_class_method of {cls.__name__}") ``` - `staticmethod`: A static method does not receive an implicit first argument. To declare a static method, use this idiom: ```python from functools import wraps def staticmethod(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): return func(*args, **kwargs) return wrapper # Example usage: class MyClass: @staticmethod def my_static_method(): print("Called my_static_method") ``` - `property`: A property provides a way of customizing access to instance attributes. To declare a property, use this idiom: ```python class MyClass: def __init__(self): self._my_attribute = None @property def my_attribute(self): return self._my_attribute @my_attribute.setter def my_attribute(self, value): self._my_attribute = value @my_attribute.deleter def my_attribute(self): del self._my_attribute ``` - `ABC`: An abstract base class is a way of defining interfaces in Python. To declare an abstract base class, use this idiom: ```python from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class MyAbstractClass(ABC): @abstractmethod def my_abstract_method(self): pass ``` - `abstractmethod`: An abstract method is a method that has a declaration but does not have an implementation. To declare an abstract method, use this idiom: ```python from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class MyAbstractClass(ABC): @abstractmethod def my_abstract_method(self): pass ``` - `dataclass`: A dataclass is a class that is primarily used to store data. To declare a dataclass, use the `@dataclass` decorator: ```python from dataclasses import dataclass @dataclass class MyDataClass: my_attribute: int ``` Note: The `classmethod`, `staticmethod`, and `property` examples are using custom decorators for demonstration purposes. In practice, you would use the built-in decorators provided by Python (`@classmethod`, `@staticmethod`, `@property`).

data_pointers.title: 类高级功能
data_pointers.content_preview: 类高级功能: English translation: - `classmethod`: A class method receives the class as implicit first argument, just like an instance method receives the instance. To declare a class method, use this idiom: ```python from functools import wraps def classmethod(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(cls, *args, **kwargs): return func(cls, *args, **kwargs) return wrapper # Example usage: class MyClass: @classmethod def my_class_method(cls): print(f"Called my_class_method of {cls.__name__}") ``` - `staticmethod`: A static method does not receive an implicit first argument. To declare a static method, use this idiom: ```python from functools import wraps def staticmethod(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): return func(*args, **kwargs) return wrapper # Example usage: class MyClass: @staticmethod def my_static_method(): print("Called my_static_method") ``` - `property`: A property provides a way of customizing access to instance attributes. To declare a property, use this idiom: ```python class MyClass: def __init__(self): self._my_attribute = None @property def my_attribute(self): return self._my_attribute @my_attribute.setter def my_attribute(self, value): self._my_attribute = value @my_attribute.deleter def my_attribute(self): del self._my_attribute ``` - `ABC`: An abstract base class is a way of defining interfaces in Python. To declare an abstract base class, use this idiom: ```python from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class MyAbstractClass(ABC): @abstractmethod def my_abstract_method(self): pass ``` - `abstractmethod`: An abstract method is a method that has a declaration but does not have an implementation. To declare an abstract method, use this idiom: ```python from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class MyAbstractClass(ABC): @abstractmethod def my_abstract_method(self): pass ``` - `dataclass`: A dataclass is a class that is primarily used to store data. To declare a dataclass, use the `@dataclass` decorator: ```python from dataclasses import dataclass @dataclass class MyDataClass: my_attribute: int ``` Note: The `classmethod`, `staticmethod`, and `property` examples are using custom decorators for demonstration purposes. In practice, you would use the built-in decorators provided by Python (`@classmethod`, `@staticmethod`, `@property`).
